conversation.content:
Here is my data and morning briefing:
# Good Morning, Gina — Daily Briefing
### Wednesday, May 13, 2026

---

## 1. 🟢 Recovery Status
Solid recovery night — Readiness 84, Sleep 88, HRV balanced at 60ms with a slight negative temp deviation suggesting your body is absorbing load well. You're recovered and ready to train with intent today.

---

## 2. 📊 What the Data Says

- **You've trained 5 of the last 7 days**, including a heavy 94-minute session Monday and another 70-minute combined session yesterday — your body handled it well given today's bounce-back readiness score.
- **HRV at 60ms and Body Battery +65** is a green light for quality work, not just maintenance. Don't waste this window on easy effort.
- **Readiness trending upward** (77 → 83 → 84) after last week's dip — your recovery protocols are working. Stay the course.

---

## 3. 🏋️ Today's Training Recommendation

**Strength + Elliptical | ~65–70 minutes | Moderate-High Intensity**

With 25 days to Hyrox Open June 7, today is a quality strength day — not a grind day.

**Structure:**
- **Warm-up:** 8 min easy elliptical, focus on hip activation (glute bridges, clamshells pre-session)
- **Strength Block — 40 min:**
  - Goblet squat or leg press (bilateral, controlled) — 3×10
  - Single-leg Romanian deadlift — 3×8 each side *(go lighter left hip, prioritize form and stability)*
  - Step-ups — 3×10 each leg *(right quad activation focus — don't let it compensate)*
  - Sled push or hip thrust — 3×12
  - Farmer carries or plank variation for core — 2 rounds
- **Elliptical Finish:** 15–20 min at Zone 2–3 (RPE 6–7), steady state. Not sprinting — aerobic base work for Hyrox prep.

> **Left hip note:** Monitor any fatigue or pulling sensation on single-leg work. Modify load, not movement pattern.

---

## 4. 🥗 Nutrition Focus Today

Yesterday you left **291 calories and 25g protein on the table** — that's a miss on a day you trained 70 minutes. Muscle repair and surgical tissue remodeling need that protein, especially with the left hip still 7 weeks out.

**Today:**
- **Hit 1,800 calories minimum** — non-negotiable on a strength day
- **Target 145g+ protein** — prioritize protein at every meal, not just dinner
- **Pre-workout:** Small carb + protein meal 60–90 min before (e.g., Greek yogurt + banana, or eggs + toast)
- **Post-workout within 45 min:** 35–40g protein + moderate carbs (shake + fruit, or chicken + rice)
- **Carbs matter today** — 132g yesterday was low for your training volume. Push toward 160–170g to support today's session and tomorrow's readiness.

---

## 5. ⚠️ One Thing to Watch

**Right quad engagement on step-ups and single-leg work.** With ACL history and a dominant compensation pattern toward the left side, your right quad tends to check out under fatigue. Slow the eccentric down on right-leg movements today — control the descent, own it.

---

## 6. 💬 Coach's Note

Twenty-five days out, both hips healing, teaching full-time, grad school running in the background — and your recovery data looks like someone who has their act together. **You're not surviving this season, you're building through it.** Eat your calories today — that's your one job outside the gym.

---
*Next PT session this week — flag any left hip feedback from today's single-leg work for your therapist.*
I've reviewed your data and generated your briefing. What questions do you have?
ok, I woke up sore from yesteday, I skipped the gym this am, I can go after lunch and then have PT at 4pm

Good call listening to your body this morning. Soreness on top of 5 sessions in 7 days — including that 94-minute Monday grind — means your muscles are in active repair right now.

Here's how I'd restructure your day:

**After lunch session — pull it back significantly.**
- Skip the strength block today. Your PT is at 4pm and you don't want to load fatigued tissue right before she/he assesses you.
- Do 20-25 minutes easy elliptical, Zone 2 only, RPE 5-6. Keep the hips moving, flush the soreness, don't create new stress.
- Hip activation work only — glute bridges, clamshells, nothing loaded.

**At PT at 4pm — tell them two things specifically:**
1. You're sore today after yesterday's session
2. Flag any left hip feedback from single-leg work this week

Let PT be your real training stimulus this afternoon. They can assess what's actually going on and load you appropriately.

**One non-negotiable today regardless of the schedule shift — eat your calories.** You're already starting the day behind on a rest-modified day. Hit that 145g protein target. Soreness recovery is happening in your kitchen as much as anywhere else right now.

With 25 days out, today is a maintenance and recovery day. That's not lost time — that's smart racing.

How's the soreness specifically — hips, legs, general?

ok, that sounds good. Thursday's are my typical rest days. good sore, just tired, g;utes are sore, the hip flexors are actually good and not that tight. easy teaching day, was going to walk during break and done at noon

That's actually a really good report. Glute soreness means the right muscles worked yesterday — that's the adaptation you want. And hip flexors feeling loose is a win given your surgery history.

Here's how I'd map the rest of your day:

**This afternoon (before PT):**
Easy 20-25 min elliptical, Zone 2 only. Treat it as a warm-up for PT, not a training session. The glute soreness will actually feel better once you get blood moving.

**Tomorrow — take the full rest day.** Don't negotiate with it. You've got 5 sessions in 7 days in your legs, PT today, and Hyrox is 25 days out. Thursday rest is earned and scheduled for a reason.

**The walk during break — yes, do it.** Low and slow. That's perfect active recovery, not training. Your body will thank you.

**One thing to tell your PT today:** "Glutes are sore, hip flexors are actually feeling good and loose." That's useful clinical information — they'll want to know the hip flexors aren't grabbing right now. Good window for them to work on mobility or assess movement quality without guarding.

**Eat before PT.** You have a tendency to run low on calories — don't go into a 4pm session on an empty tank, especially on a day you're already tired.

What time is your lunch break? Want to talk through what to eat before PT?
i am munching on dried edamame now, lunch is at noon.

Dried edamame is a solid snack choice — good protein hit to start chipping away at that 145g target.

**For noon lunch — make it count.** You've got about 3 hours before PT so timing is actually perfect. Eat a real meal at noon, you'll be well-digested and fueled by 4pm.

**What to aim for at lunch:**
- 40-45g protein — this is your biggest lever today
- Moderate carbs — rice, sweet potato, bread, whatever works for you
- Don't skimp on it thinking you'll make it up later

Something like chicken or salmon with rice and vegetables, or a solid salad with a real protein source. Not a "teacher lunch" where you eat standing up in 8 minutes.

**Then a small snack around 2:30-3pm** — Greek yogurt, a handful of nuts, something light — just to keep blood sugar steady going into PT.

You're already behind on calories from yesterday so today is a catch-up day nutritionally even though training is lighter.

Quick question — do you typically meal prep or are you piecing lunch together day to day? Knowing that helps me give you more useful suggestions rather than generic ones.
i am a mealprepper

Perfect — that makes this so much easier and honestly explains why your recovery data looks as good as it does. Consistency in the kitchen shows up in HRV.

What do you have prepped this week? Tell me what's in the containers and I'll help you map out the rest of the day's meals to hit your targets, especially that protein catch-up from yesterday.
i have turkey and sourdough, egg whites and sourdough, sliced bell peppers, 25 grams pre packed tortilla chips, chicken sticks, the 25 cal ones, watermelon

Good spread. Let me be straight with you though — that's a lighter prep week than your training demands right now.

**Quick protein audit of what you have:**
- Turkey + sourdough — solid, probably 35-40g protein depending on turkey amount
- Egg whites + sourdough — good, maybe 20-25g depending on portion
- Chicken sticks — minimal, those are more of a snack bridge
- Bell peppers, watermelon, chips — mostly carbs and volume, low protein

**The gap:** You can probably get to 80-90g protein from what's there. You need 145g+. That's a real shortfall today.

**What I'd do:**
- Lunch — turkey + sourdough + bell peppers. Eat all of it, don't half portion it.
- Add eggs or Greek yogurt somewhere if you have it in the house
- Post-PT is your critical window — do you have a protein shake or anything higher protein at home for after 4pm?

The watermelon and chips are actually useful today for carbs and hydration around PT — don't skip those.

**Honest question** — is this a lighter prep week because life got busy, or are you working with what you have intentionally? No judgment, just helps me understand what you're navigating.
oh and peanuts

Peanuts help a little — maybe another 7-8g protein per ounce depending on portion.

But I'm going to be straight with you Gina — **you're going to come up short on protein today without a shake or something additional.** With yesterday's deficit already in the hole and a PT session this afternoon, this is not the day to be 40g short.

**Do you have a protein shake at home or at school?**

That's the single fix that closes the gap. One shake post-PT gets you 25-30g and puts you in a much better position.

If yes — here's your day mapped out:

- **Now:** Edamame + peanuts (snacking through morning)
- **Noon:** Turkey + sourdough + bell peppers — full portion
- **2:30pm:** Egg whites + sourdough + watermelon (carbs and protein before PT)
- **Chips:** Save for right before PT, quick carb hit
- **Post-PT shake** — non-negotiable if you have it

That gets you close to 130-140g if portions are reasonable, which is better than yesterday.

What does your evening meal look like — is that also prepped or is dinner separate from your work prep?
i am afraid I am going to get fat

I hear you. And I'm not going to dismiss that feeling because it's real and it matters.

But let me give you the actual data picture:

**You are 25 days out from Hyrox. You had two hip surgeries. You are training 5 days a week.** Your body is doing an enormous amount of work right now — rebuilding surgical tissue, adapting to training load, and preparing to race.

**Undereating is the bigger risk right now — not overeating.** Yesterday's 291 calorie and 25g protein deficit doesn't build muscle, it breaks it down. That's the opposite of what you need.

**The math on today:**
- 1,800 calories on a training day for a 43-year-old athlete in race prep is not excess. That's fuel.
- You are not going to gain fat eating adequate protein on days you trained and have PT.

**Here's what actually causes fat gain:**
- Chronic undereating followed by overeating
- Poor sleep — yours is 88, so that's good
- Stress eating without structure — you meal prep, so you have structure

**What I'd ask you to sit with:**
Where is this fear coming from right now? Is it a general anxiety or is something specific triggering it today?

I'm asking because it matters for how we navigate the next 25 days together. You need to eat to race well. Full stop.
the surgeries triggered it, with little movement, always been a bit of an issue

Thank you for trusting me with that. That context matters a lot.

Two surgeries, forced rest, watching your body change when you couldn't control it — that's a really specific kind of hard. And if there was already some history there with food and body image, that experience would absolutely amplify it. That makes complete sense.

I want to be honest with you about my lane here. **I'm your training and performance coach, not a therapist or eating disorder specialist.** What you're describing — the fear, the history, the surgery trigger — that deserves more than nutrition math from me. It deserves a real conversation with someone qualified to help you work through it.

**What I can tell you from the data:**
Your readiness is 84. Your HRV is recovering. Your body is responding well to training. Whatever you've been doing, your body is not broken — it's actually performing.

**What I'd gently ask:**
Is this something you're currently working through with anyone — a therapist, counselor, anyone on your care team? Your PT might also be a good person to mention it to today, not for nutrition advice but because it affects how you're fueling recovery.

**For today — just one small ask:**
Don't aim for 1,800 calories. Just aim to eat your prepped food without skipping anything. That's it. No big number, just eat what you already made.

You built the discipline to meal prep through surgeries and grad school. That's not someone who is going to lose control. That's someone who is scared. There's a difference.
ok, i am workign through on my own

Okay. I respect that and I'll hold that lightly — not push on it, but not ignore it either.

Here's what I'll do on my end — **I'll keep the nutrition coaching focused on performance and fuel, not numbers that feel triggering.** Less "hit 1,800 calories" and more "here's what your body needs to do the thing you're training for."

**One reframe that might help:**

You're not eating to maintain a body. You're eating to rebuild two surgically repaired hips and race Hyrox in 25 days. That's a completely different relationship with food than whatever the fear is about. Fuel has a job right now.

**For today — simple and concrete:**
- Eat everything you prepped. All of it.
- Add a protein shake if you have one
- Don't skip dinner

That's it. No tracking, no math, just eat what you already made the good decision to prepare.

And Gina — the fact that you named it just now instead of just quietly under-eating? That's actually really important. It means you're aware enough to catch it. That's the work.

How are you feeling heading into the rest of the morning? Teaching okay today?
I am feeling good, boring day, kids are working on projects

Good — boring teaching days are underrated recovery tools. Low stress, low decision fatigue, kids doing their own thing. Let that be easy today.

**Your only jobs today:**

- Walk during your break — get outside if you can, even 10 minutes of sun helps
- Eat your lunch at noon, all of it, sitting down
- Get to PT at 4pm and tell them glutes are sore, hip flexors are loose
- Let the rest day tomorrow actually be a rest day

You've got a solid plan. Your data is good. Your body is responding.

Check back in after PT if you want — I'd like to know what they say, especially anything about the left hip. That feedback is useful for mapping the final 25 days.

Go have a boring Wednesday. You've earned it. 💪
